page: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It features an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance provides 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include any local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core results. However, memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with both small-block and large-block read-only bandwidth testing in the bottom 25%. In application-specific benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong text processing capabilities and average performance in static web serving, Redis SET operations, and small-model LLM inference. Conversely, it shows weaker results in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4as_v6 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for standard workloads without the overhead of bundled local storage or specialized accelerators. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, text processing, and lightweight application hosting. It is less optimal for memory-bandwidth-intensive tasks, large-scale database operations, or complex multi-threaded computations like software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it offers 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores and is paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. The instance features no bundled local storage or GPU resources but is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core capabilities, particularly in text processing, alongside average multi-core and web serving throughput. However, memory bandwidth and database operations represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower percentiles. This configuration provides a balanced, cost-efficient resource profile for general-purpose workloads, web hosting, and light application tasks, while remaining less suited for memory-intensive or heavy database applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 4 dedicated vCPUs, and 16.0 GB of RAM, optimized for balanced workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, ideal for web serving and text processing.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring strong single-core performance and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for standard application workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and no bundled local storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Shows moderate performance for static web serving and Redis operations
Best suited for general-purpose applications and light web hosting
